commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f084fed82e1445116b3180b26ea1fddc33494cd6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Slack inbound routing so top-level direct messages with `replyToMode=all` receive per-message thread-scoped session keys, with one regression test and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence real reproduction is available. Source and tests show current main keeps two top-level Slack DMs from the same user on the same direct session, but the PR does not include a real Slack overlapping-DM proof.
commands.review_followup: A Slack routing owner should decide the DM session contract before repair because the current branch fixes overlap by changing existing user-visible session behavior.

target.title: fix(slack): isolate top-level DM thread sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate top-level Slack DM turns into Slack-thread scoped sessions when `replyToMode=all`.
- Keep `replyToMode=off` and true Slack thread reply behavior unchanged.
- Add a regression test covering two overlapping top-level DMs from the same user.

## Verification

- Confirmed latest `origin/main` still keeps top-level DMs on `agent:main:slack:direct:<user>` and has a test asserting that old behavior.
- Watched the new regression test fail before the production change: expected `agent:main:slack:direct:u3:thread:1770408530.000000`, received `agent:main:slack:direct:u3`.
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/threading.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: guanbear

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
